markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games has delayed Grand Theft Auto VI multiple times, with the current target of November 19, 2026, following Take-Two Interactive’s earnings updates that confirmed the shift from earlier 2025 and spring 2026 windows. This extended timeline fuels trader focus on high-profile developments that could resolve before launch, including major AI model releases, music album drops, and geopolitical shifts. Precursor signals such as ongoing marketing campaigns and platform metrics shape expectations, while the market’s substantial volume reflects broad interest in how industry cycles, streaming trends, and cultural milestones align with the game’s release window. Upcoming earnings calls and any new trailer or promotional activity remain key near-term catalysts.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York retrial on the Jessica Mann rape charge stands as the dominant factor behind the market’s heavy weighting toward no additional prison time. A hung jury on May 15, 2026, left prosecutors with until late June to decide whether to pursue a fourth trial, while Weinstein continues serving a 16-year California sentence from 2022 that remains under appeal. At 74 and dealing with documented health issues, the former producer’s legal team has highlighted procedural challenges and jury fatigue across multiple proceedings. Traders appear to view the stalled New York case and ongoing appeals as significant barriers to further convictions that could extend his incarceration, with any retrial decision or appellate ruling expected to clarify the outlook in the coming weeks.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights have reached the 2026 Stanley Cup Final after dominant regular-season campaigns, with Carolina posting a 53-22-7 record as Metropolitan Division and Eastern Conference champions. Vegas claimed the Pacific Division title and advanced through the Western bracket on strong goaltending and depth. A 5-4 double-overtime win in Game 3 gave Vegas a 2-1 series lead heading into Game 4, keeping implied probabilities tightly contested around the current trader consensus. Both clubs enter with proven playoff resilience and favorable rest patterns from earlier rounds, sustaining their elevated positioning for the championship.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks hold a 2-1 lead in the 2026 NBA Finals against the San Antonio Spurs, with Game 4 scheduled at Madison Square Garden, positioning them as the market favorite at 62.5% implied probability. New York’s strong regular-season finish at 53-29, bolstered by Jalen Brunson’s consistent scoring and the team’s defensive efficiency, helped secure home-court advantage after advancing through the Eastern Conference. The Spurs, who posted a 62-20 record led by Victor Wembanyama’s dominant two-way play, narrowed the series with a Game 3 road win that briefly lengthened New York’s odds. Trader consensus reflects the Knicks’ series lead and immediate home matchup, while acknowledging San Antonio’s potential to force a longer series through Wembanyama’s impact and roster depth in a tightly contested championship.

The Spurs, who posted a 62-20 record led by Victor Wembanyama’s dominant two-way play, narrowed the series with a Game 3 road win that briefly lengthened New York’s odds. Trader consensus reflects the Knicks’ series lead and immediate home matchup, while acknowledging San Antonio’s potential to force a longer series through Wembanyama’s impact and roster depth in a tightly contested championship.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party, state institutions, and military through loyalist appointments and ongoing elite reshuffles, including recent military purges that have extended into his inner circle as of early 2026. No designated successor has emerged, and repeated rumors of health issues, factional challenges, or removal have not materialized into verifiable threats. Analysts across sources expect him to secure a fourth term at the 21st Party Congress in late 2027, consistent with the precedent of eliminated presidential term limits. This positioning underpins the strong trader consensus against his ouster before the end of 2026, though unforeseen events such as sudden illness or elite realignments ahead of the congress could still shift dynamics.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus in the wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential nomination field at 23.5 percent, reflecting his executive record as California governor, national name recognition, fundraising infrastructure, and high-profile policy clashes with the current administration. The remaining field stays fragmented below 9 percent, with Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Kamala Harris, and Jon Ossoff representing distinct progressive, establishment, and Senate profiles. Key differentiators include state-level governance experience versus legislative or national profiles, perceived strength in battleground states, and positioning on economic and regulatory issues. Consolidation behind one contender could accelerate after the 2026 midterms, through major endorsements, early primary results, or polling shifts that clarify voter coalitions ahead of the convention.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus in the wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential nomination field at 23.5 percent, reflecting his executive record as California governor, national name recognition, fundraising infrastructure, and high-profile policy clashes with the current administration. The remaining field stays fragmented below 9 percent, with Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Kamala Harris, and Jon Ossoff representing distinct progressive, establishment, and Senate profiles. Key differentiators include state-level governance experience versus legislative or national profiles, perceived strength in battleground states, and positioning on economic and regulatory issues. Consolidation behind one contender could accelerate after the 2026 midterms, through major endorsements, early primary results, or polling shifts that clarify voter coalitions ahead of the convention.
